The story arc (5 parts).label: Opening
The story arc (5 parts).text: The whistle blew, and I froze at the starting line with my heart hammering against my ribs.

The story arc (5 parts).label: Rising action
The story arc (5 parts).text: With every step, the gap between me and the leading runner stretched wider, and my legs began to ache.

The story arc (5 parts).label: Climax
The story arc (5 parts).text: As the finish line loomed, I dug deep, lunged forward, and felt my chest brush the tape a heartbeat before my rival.

The story arc (5 parts).label: Falling action
The story arc (5 parts).text: I staggered to a stop, gasping, while my teammates rushed onto the track towards me.

The story arc (5 parts).label: Resolution
The story arc (5 parts).text: I had not believed I could win, but as the medal settled around my neck, I realised that effort mattered more than talent.

Show, don't tell.label: TELL: He was very angry.
Show, don't tell.text: SHOW: He slammed his fist on the desk and his face turned a deep, blotchy red.

Show, don't tell.label: TELL: The room was messy.
Show, don't tell.text: SHOW: Crumpled clothes spilled from the drawers and half-eaten snacks littered the floor.

Show, don't tell.label: TELL: She was nervous before the exam.
Show, don't tell.text: SHOW: She chewed her pencil, read the same question three times, and felt her stomach twist into knots.

Show, don't tell.label: TELL: The old man was tired.
Show, don't tell.text: SHOW: The old man's shoulders sagged, and he lowered himself onto the bench with a long, weary sigh.

Show, don't tell.label: TELL: It was a happy reunion.
Show, don't tell.text: SHOW: They flung their arms around each other, laughing and crying at the same time.

Five senses description.label: Sound
Five senses description.text: The market roared with the clatter of pans, the haggling of shoppers, and the sizzle of frying noodles.

Five senses description.label: Smell
Five senses description.text: A sharp, sour stench rose from the rubbish bins and clung to the back of my throat.

Five senses description.label: Touch
Five senses description.text: The damp cave wall was cold and slimy beneath my fingertips, and a chill crept up my arm.

Five senses description.label: Taste
Five senses description.text: The first bite of the mango was so sweet and juicy that the syrup dribbled down my chin.

Five senses description.label: Sight
Five senses description.text: Golden sunlight spilled across the field, turning the dew on every blade of grass into tiny sparkling jewels.

Read-aloud.label: The Lost Key
Read-aloud.text: Mei Ling searched everywhere for her house key. She checked her bag, her pockets, and even under the doormat. Just as she was about to give up, she heard a soft jingle. The key had slipped into the lining of her schoolbag all along. She sighed with relief and hurried inside before the rain began to fall.

Read-aloud.label: A Morning at the Hawker Centre
Read-aloud.text: The hawker centre buzzed with life. Steam rose from bowls of noodles, and the smell of grilled chicken filled the air. Uncle Tan called out the day's special while plates clattered behind him. I found a seat by the window and watched the busy crowd, my stomach rumbling with hunger.

Read-aloud.label: The Storm
Read-aloud.text: The sky darkened in an instant. A cold wind whipped through the trees, bending their branches like reeds. Then the rain came, hammering against the windows and drumming on the roof. I pressed my face to the glass and watched a flash of lightning split the clouds, followed by a crash of thunder that rattled the whole house. Somehow, safe inside, I felt a thrill rather than fear.

Read-aloud.label: The Decision
Read-aloud.text: Arjun stood at the edge of the diving board, his toes curled over the cold metal. Far below, the pool shimmered, and dozens of faces turned up to watch him. His heart pounded and his mouth went dry. He could climb back down, or he could leap. Taking one last breath, he closed his eyes and flung himself forward into the waiting blue.
